user.title: Pac-Man
user.description: The ghosts are loose in your smart home! Set up Pac-Man to chase them down with a playable card right on your dashboard. Waka waka waka!

abort.already_configured: Pac-Man is already chomping away on your system. There can only be one Pac-Man!

state.original_1980: Pac-Man was created by Toru Iwatani and released by Namco in Japan on May 22, 1980.
state.namco_creation: Pac-Man was developed by Namco and was originally called 'Puck Man' in Japan.
state.toru_iwatani: Toru Iwatani, the creator of Pac-Man, was inspired by the image of a pizza with a slice removed.
state.pizza_inspiration: The shape of Pac-Man was inspired by a pizza with one slice taken out of it.
state.four_ghosts: The four ghosts are Blinky (red), Pinky (pink), Inky (cyan), and Clyde (orange).
state.blinky_shadow: Blinky, the red ghost, always chases Pac-Man directly.
state.pinky_speedy: Pinky, the pink ghost, tries to ambush Pac-Man by targeting four tiles ahead.
state.inky_bashful: Inky, the cyan ghost, uses both Blinky's position and Pac-Man's direction to set its target.
state.clyde_pokey: Clyde, the orange ghost, chases when far from Pac-Man but retreats to its corner when close.
state.power_pellet: Eating a Power Pellet temporarily turns the ghosts blue, allowing Pac-Man to eat them.
state.fruit_bonus: Bonus fruit items appear twice per level in the center of the maze, worth bonus points.
state.perfect_score: The maximum score in a perfect game of Pac-Man is 3,333,360 points.
state.kill_screen: Pac-Man has a 'kill screen' at level 256 where the right half of the maze becomes garbled.
state.maze_patterns: Expert players use pre-planned movement patterns to avoid ghosts without power pellets.
state.ghost_house: The ghost house in the center of the maze is where the ghosts start each life.
state.tunnel_speed: Ghosts slow down when passing through the tunnels on the sides of the maze.
state.scatter_mode: Ghosts periodically enter 'scatter mode' and retreat to their designated corners.
state.chase_mode: In 'chase mode', each ghost pursues Pac-Man using its unique targeting strategy.
state.frightened_mode: After eating a power pellet, ghosts enter 'frightened mode' and turn blue.
state.elroy_mode: When few dots remain, Blinky speeds up. This was called 'Cruise Elroy' by Midway staff.
state.ms_pacman: Ms. Pac-Man (1982) introduced moving fruit and randomised ghost behavior.
state.highest_grossing: Pac-Man was the highest-grossing arcade game of the 1980s.
state.cultural_icon: Pac-Man became one of the most recognizable pop culture icons of all time.
state.pac_man_fever: The 1982 novelty song 'Pac-Man Fever' reached number 9 on the US Billboard Hot 100.
state.japan_1980: In Japan, Pac-Man was released on May 22, 1980 under the name 'Puck Man'.
state.us_1980: Pac-Man was released in North America in October 1980, renamed from 'Puck Man'.
state.ghost_names_japan: The Japanese ghost names are Oikake (Chaser), Machibuse (Ambusher), Kimagure (Fickle), and Otoboke (Stupid).
state.255_levels: Pac-Man has 255 playable levels before the kill screen at level 256.
state.first_mascot: Pac-Man is widely considered the first video game character to become a pop culture mascot.
state.cabinet_design: The original Pac-Man arcade cabinet was yellow to match the character.
state.sound_effects: Pac-Man's 'waka waka' sound was created by speeding up a voice saying the syllables.
state.intermissions: Pac-Man features animated intermissions between certain levels showing comedic skits.
state.no_end: The original Pac-Man has no true ending — it loops continuously until the kill screen.
state.speed_increase: Pac-Man and the ghosts both get faster as levels progress.
state.pink_ghosts: In early Pac-Man designs, all the ghosts were originally the same color.
state.waka_sound: The 'waka' sound Pac-Man makes when eating dots is one of the most recognizable sounds in gaming history.
state.game_over: The game over screen in Pac-Man simply shows the text 'GAME OVER' in pink.
state.continues: The original arcade Pac-Man does not have a continue feature — once you're out of lives, it's over.
state.millennium_arcade: Pac-Man was voted the most popular arcade game of the millennium in multiple polls.
state.smithsonian: The original Pac-Man arcade cabinet is preserved in the Smithsonian Institution.
state.moma: Pac-Man is one of the video games included in the Museum of Modern Art's permanent collection.
state.genome_project: Scientists have used Pac-Man as a metaphor and visual representation in genome research.
state.google_doodle: Google celebrated Pac-Man's 30th anniversary with a fully playable Pac-Man Google Doodle.
state.guinness_records: Pac-Man holds multiple Guinness World Records, including most successful coin-operated game.
